data.title: xAI, led by Elon Musk, is suing Colorado for limiting AI speech, claiming it restricts freedom by imposing political bias on its AI chatbot Grok.
data.description: The artificial intelligence company stated that implementing these regulations would require them to provide specific data to their AI chatbot Grok that aligns with Colorado's political beliefs, rather than focusing on being as truthful and accurate as possible.

data.title: Digital subscription results of AS Ekspress Grupp for the first quarter of 2026
data.description: In the Baltic States, the digital subscriptions for AS Ekspress Grupp experienced a 6% increase compared to the previous year in the first quarter, which had seen a decrease of 3%. By the end of March, the total number of digital subscriptions reached 237,136.

data.title: Idorsia issues invitation to the 2026 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders
data.description: Allschwil in Switzerland saw Idorsia Ltd (SIX: IDIA) announce the call for the upcoming Annual General Meeting (AGM) on April 10, 2026. Shareholders are invited to convene on Wednesday, May 6, 2026, at 14.00 CEST at the Congress Center, Messe Basel, Switzerland. The meeting's agenda includes the approval of the Annual Report for the year ending December 31, 2025.

data.title: Fagron schedules Ordinary and Extraordinary General Meeting for May 11, 2026.
data.description: In recent developments, it has been reported that the unemployment rate has dropped to 4.2%, which is the lowest it has been in five years. This decrease in unemployment has been attributed to the addition of 210,000 new jobs in the last month alone. Analysts suggest that this positive trend in job growth is a sign of a strengthening economy. Furthermore, the stock market has responded positively to this news, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average reaching a record high of 35,000 points. Overall, these indicators point towards a promising outlook for the country's economic recovery.

data.title: AS Tallinna Sadam operational volumes for 2026 Q1
data.description: In the first quarter of 2026, Tallinna Sadam witnessed the transit of 3.1 million tons of cargo and 1.4 million passengers. Compared to the prior year, cargo volumes saw a decline of 8%, equating to 265 thousand tonnes less. The reduction was primarily driven by a substantial drop in liquid bulk due to challenging sea ice conditions. However, general cargo surged by an impressive 57%, translating to an increase of 66 thousand tonnes, while ro-ro cargo saw a modest uptick of 2%, equivalent to 31 thousand tonnes. Passenger numbers dipped by 3.3%, accounting for 47 thousand fewer passengers, with ship maintenance activities impacting passenger operations. Vessel traffic overall decreased by 7%. Notably, vehicle crossings between the Estonian mainland and the main islands rose by 0.9%, while passenger numbers fell by 1.2%. The icebreaker Botnica maintained the same number of charter days as the previous year, operating at full capacity with a utilization rate of 100%.

data.title: EfTEN Real Estate Fund AS’s financial results for Q1 2026 and net asset value as of 31 March 2026
data.description: In the first quarter of 2026, EfTEN Real Estate Fund AS reported a total consolidated rental income of €8.180 million, marking a 6.5% rise from the prior year. The Fund's consolidated EBITDA for the same period reached €6.817 million, indicating a 10.3% increase year-on-year. EBITDA saw a 12% boost in the retail segment, a 10% rise in the logistics segment, and a significant 63% surge in the elderly care segment, whereas the office segment maintained EBITDA at the previous year's level.

data.title: First six months of FY 2026: initial management actions initiated; revised full-year guidance.
data.description: The recent report revealed a significant increase in unemployment rates, with over 10 million people now out of work. The latest figures show a 3% rise in joblessness, bringing the total to 7.5% of the population. The hardest-hit sectors were hospitality and retail, experiencing a 5% surge in layoffs. Additionally, there was a notable decrease in job vacancies, down by 20% compared to last year. Experts attribute this spike in unemployment to the ongoing economic downturn caused by the global pandemic. Moreover, the government is facing mounting pressure to provide support and aid to those affected by the crisis, as the situation continues to worsen.

data.title: New pricing model introduced by x402 protocol for AI compute requests, transitioning from flat fees to usage-based pricing to cater to LLM inference, compute, and data queries.
data.description: The transition from fixed charges to flexible pricing aims to facilitate the utilization of artificial intelligence (AI) agents for LLM inference, computing, and data search.

data.title: Researcher finds a way to make Bitcoin quantum-safe without protocol upgrade; $75-$150 per transaction in GPU power.
data.description: Despite its potential benefits, the utilization of this system may become costly for users, ranging between $75 and $150 per transaction in terms of GPU computing power. This could restrict its viability for widespread use.

data.title: Pony.ai Launches PonyWorld 2.0, a Self-Improving Physical AI Engine for Autonomous Driving
data.description: Pony AI Inc. has introduced an enhanced world model that empowers AI technology to self-diagnose shortcomings, facilitate focused data gathering, and advance the upcoming stage of broad L4 commercialization. The company, based in Guangzhou, China, announced this development on April 10, 2026. Pony AI, a prominent player in the sector, is listed on the NASDAQ under the symbol PONY and on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange as 2026.
